@@ -0,0 +1,139 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a580
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Fix: Enter on focused perspective tab does not start inline rename'
+---
+## What
+
+Pressing **Enter** on a perspective tab that is the spatial-focused element does nothing — the inline rename editor never appears. Double-clicking the tab works (the `onDoubleClick → startRename(p.id)` path in `perspective-tab-bar.tsx`), but there is no keyboard equivalent. The keyboard path is broken because nothing binds Enter to the rename trigger when focus is in a perspective scope.
+
+### Why it's broken
+
+The rename trigger is the `triggerStartRename()` module-level broadcaster in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx`. It is invoked by exactly one caller — the `ui.entity.startRename` command in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`:
+
+```yaml
+- id: ui.entity.startRename
+  name: Rename Perspective
+```
+
+That command has **no `keys:` block** and no `scope:` constraint. Nothing keyboard-side fires it. The companion `perspective.rename` command in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml` carries `keys: {}` (explicitly empty). So:
+
+- Palette → Enter executes `ui.entity.startRename` → calls `triggerStartRename()` → tab enters rename mode (works today).
+- Mouse double-click on tab → calls `startRename(id)` directly (works today).
+- Keyboard Enter on a spatially-focused perspective tab → falls through to the global drill-in binding (which is a no-op for a leaf scope) → nothing happens (the bug).
+
+### Fix shape
+
+Add a per-scope keyboard binding that fires the start-rename trigger when the focused element's command scope chain contains a perspective entity. The binding must shadow the global drill-in Enter for that scope only — drilling-in on a leaf perspective tab has no semantic meaning, but renaming does.
+
+Two reasonable seams; pick the global maximum:
+
+1. **Add `keys` + `scope` to `ui.entity.startRename`** in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml`:
+
+   ```yaml
+   - id: ui.entity.startRename
+     name: Rename Perspective
+     scope: "entity:perspective"
+     keys:
+       cua: Enter
+       vim: Enter
+       emacs: Enter
+   ```
+
+   This is the simplest patch and reuses the existing `extractScopeBindings` walk in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts` (already verified by `keybindings.test.ts`) to surface the binding only when focus is in a perspective scope. **Trade-off**: the command name "Rename Perspective" is misleading — `ui.entity.*` reads as "any entity," but the only renameable entity today is perspective. If a second renameable entity ships later, this binding broadens or has to fork.
+
+2. **Add a perspective-scoped alias `perspective.startRename`** in `perspective.yaml` whose execute simply dispatches `ui.entity.startRename`:
+
+   ```yaml
+   - id: perspective.startRename
+     name: Rename Perspective
+     scope: "entity:perspective"
+     visible: false
+     keys:
+       cua: Enter
+       vim: Enter
+       emacs: Enter
+   ```
+
+   With its execute path wired to `triggerStartRename()` (or to an internal dispatch of `ui.entity.startRename`). This keeps the `ui.entity.*` namespace generic and concentrates the "perspective tab Enter = rename" decision in the perspective domain. **Trade-off**: a second registry entry that does the same thing.
+
+The implementer chooses based on which framing matches the architecture better. The user-visible behavior must match either way: Enter on a focused perspective tab enters inline rename mode.
+
+### Files in scope
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/ui.yaml` (option 1) **OR** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/commands/perspective.yaml` (option 2).
+- If option 2 is chosen: `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs` must register the new command; the Rust execute function calls a no-op (the rename is React-side via `triggerStartRename`).
+- No React changes should be required — `extractScopeBindings` already wires per-scope keys into the global key handler. Verify by running the test suite.
+
+### What this task does NOT do
+
+- It does not change the rename **commit** path (Enter inside the CM6 editor → `dispatchPerspectiveRename`). That already works (`perspective-tab-bar.tsx` `useInlineRenamePolicy` builds `buildSubmitCancelExtensions` with `alwaysSubmitOnEnter: true`).
+- It does not touch the double-click path.
+- It does not extend rename to other entity types.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Pressing Enter (in cua / vim normal / emacs) while a perspective tab is the spatial-focused element flips the tab into inline rename mode — the `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts in place of the tab name.
+- [ ] The behavior shadows the global drill-in Enter binding only inside the perspective scope; pressing Enter on a focused board / column / card still does its existing drill-in.
+- [ ] Pressing Enter on a perspective tab when no perspective is the active perspective is a no-op (mirrors `triggerStartRename`'s existing guard: `if (activePerspective) startRename(activePerspective.id)`).
+- [ ] After typing a new name and pressing Enter inside the rename editor, `perspective.rename` is dispatched with `{ id, new_name }` — i.e. the existing commit path keeps working unmodified.
+- [ ] Pressing Escape inside the rename editor cancels (cua/emacs) or commits (vim normal) per existing policy.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` is green.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` are green.
+
+## Tests
+
+### Browser Tests (mandatory)
+
+Run under Vitest browser mode (`vitest-browser-react` + Playwright Chromium). They prove the keyboard path works end-to-end against the real DOM, so the human user does not have to manually click around to verify.
+
+#### Test file
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.enter-rename.spatial.test.tsx` (new file)
+
+#### Setup
+
+- Mock `@tauri-apps/api/core` and `@tauri-apps/api/event` per the canonical pattern in `grid-view.nav-is-eventdriven.test.tsx` (`vi.hoisted` + `mockInvoke` + `mockListen` + `fireFocusChanged` helper).
+- Provide a perspective fixture with at least two perspectives so the test can drive focus to a specific tab.
+- Render the tab bar inside `<SpatialFocusProvider><FocusLayer name="test"><PerspectiveTabBar/></FocusLayer></SpatialFocusProvider>` plus the providers `PerspectiveTabBar` already requires (`PerspectivesProvider`, `ViewsProvider`, `UIStateProvider`, `SchemaProvider`, `CommandScopeProvider`).
+- Capture the registered key for the `perspective_tab:<id>` Focusable from `mockInvoke` calls so the test can fire `focus-changed` for that exact key.
+
+#### Required test cases
+
+1. **Enter triggers rename** — Spatially focus the active perspective tab via `fireFocusChanged(<perspective_tab:id>)`. Assert `[data-moniker="perspective_tab:<id>"]` carries `data-focused="true"`. Press Enter via `userEvent.keyboard("{Enter}")`. After the await, the tab's button contains a CodeMirror editor (`querySelector(".cm-editor")` is non-null) and the rendered name `<span>` is gone.
+
+2. **Enter on inactive tab is a no-op for rename** — Spatially focus a perspective tab whose `id !== activePerspective.id`. Press Enter. Assert no rename editor mounts (no `.cm-editor` inside any `[data-moniker^="perspective_tab:"]`). This locks in the existing `triggerStartRename` guard (`if (activePerspective) startRename(activePerspective.id)`).
+
+3. **Enter outside perspective scope still drills** — Spatially focus a non-perspective leaf (e.g. board column or card). Press Enter. Assert `mockInvoke` was called with `"spatial_drill_in"`, and NO `.cm-editor` mounted inside the perspective bar. This proves the new binding is scope-local and does not leak to other scopes.
+
+4. **Vim Enter** — Repeat case 1 with `keymap_mode: "vim"` set on UIState. Same outcome: rename editor mounts.
+
+5. **Emacs Enter** — Repeat case 1 with `keymap_mode: "emacs"`. Same outcome.
+
+6. **Commit path still works** — After case 1, type `" Edited"` into the editor and press Enter. Assert `mockInvoke` was called with `"perspective.rename"` (or whatever the command-dispatch invoke wraps it as) carrying `args: { id: <id>, new_name: "<original> Edited" }`. This is a regression guard: the new outer-Enter binding must not interfere with the inner editor's Enter-to-commit (the inner CM6 keymap takes precedence because the editor owns focus).
+
+7. **Escape behavior is preserved** — After case 1, type something, press Escape. In cua/emacs, the editor unmounts and the original name is restored (`mockInvoke` is NOT called with `perspective.rename`). In vim normal mode, Escape commits per existing policy.
+
+#### How to run
+
+```
+cd kanban-app/ui && npm test
+```
+
+Headless on CI. The CI workflow `.github/workflows/*.yml` already runs this command.
+
+### Backend tests
+
+- [ ] Add a Rust test in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/builtin_commands.rs` (or the equivalent in `swissarmyhammer-commands` if option 1 was taken) that asserts the chosen YAML command (`ui.entity.startRename` or `perspective.startRename`) carries the expected `keys` block for cua/vim/emacs and the expected `scope`.
+
+  Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-commands` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban`.
+
+### Source-level guard
+
+- [ ] Add or extend the existing keybindings test (`kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts`) so that `extractScopeBindings` against an `entity:perspective` scope returns `{ Enter: "<chosen-command-id>" }` for cua, vim, and emacs.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing browser test (case 1 — Enter on a focused active tab does nothing today) first, confirm it fails, then add the YAML keys block to make it pass. Then add the remaining cases and lock in the scope-local + commit-path + Escape behavior.
